steps.title: Find the multiplication factor when x increases by 1
steps.content:
When $x$ increases by 1, we go from $f(x)$ to $f(x+1)$:
$$f(x+1) = 66b^{x+1} = 66 \cdot b \cdot b^x = b(66b^x) = b \cdot f(x)$$
The function value is multiplied by $b$.

steps.title: Convert multiplication to percentage increase
steps.content:
When a quantity is multiplied by $b$, the percentage increase is $(b-1) \times 100\%$. Here's why:
$$\text{percentage increase} = \frac{\text{new} - \text{old}}{\text{old}} \times 100\% = \frac{b \cdot V - V}{V} \times 100\% = (b-1) \times 100\%$$
Therefore $c = 100(b-1)$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
